title: bloodhound
html:
bloodhound

> AD attack path visualization. Use after getting ANY domain creds to map the full kill chain.

Collection

bloodhound-python (from Linux)

bloodhound-python -c All -u 'user' -p 'pass' -d domain.htb -ns DC_IP --zip

SharpHound (from Windows)

.\SharpHound.exe -c All --zipfilename bh.zip

RustHound-CE (Kerberos-aware)

rusthound-ce -d domain.htb -u user@domain.htb -p 'pass' -n DC_IP -f DC.domain.htb -o domain.ad -k

Key Queries

- Shortest path to Domain Admins - Kerberoastable users - AS-REP roastable users - Users with DCSync rights - Outbound object control from owned principals - Shortest path from owned to high-value targets

Tips

- BH Legacy (4.x) vs BH CE — collection formats differ - Always run with -c All for complete data - Re-collect after gaining new privileges
